name: SavorOne
effective_cost: $39/yr, no sign-up bonus or credits to offset it; must be earned back through ~$1,300/yr of 3%-category spend

verdict.text: Reconsider: same categories as the no-fee Savor, now behind a $39 fee

earn_rates.category: Grocery stores (excludes superstores like Walmart & Target)
Entertainment: movies, concerts, sporting events, amusement parks
Popular streaming services: Netflix, Hulu, Disney+
Hotels, vacation rentals & rental cars, via Capital One Travel

earn_note: Same category rates as the no-fee Savor card; the only differences are this one charges $39/yr, skips the sign-up bonus, and targets fair credit (FICO roughly 580-669) rather than excellent credit. It exists as an approval path, not a better rewards card.

redemption_options.method: Statement credit / check / gift card / Amazon / PayPal

points.note: A straight cash-back currency, not miles: no transfer partners, no premium redemption to chase, always worth exactly 1¢.

transfer_partners.highlight: Cash back isn't transferable. It's a flat statement credit, check, gift card, or Capital One Travel/Entertainment redemption, always at face value.

insurance.detail: Secondary: pays after your personal auto insurance

protection_note: Identical thin protection to the no-fee Savor: secondary rental coverage and a modest $1,000-per-claim extended warranty, no purchase protection or trip coverage. You get no extra protection for the $39 fee.
rental_note: Secondary CDW only: decline the rental counter's own waiver and pay in full with this card to activate it, but your personal auto policy pays first.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Discover network transition
services.detail: New SavorOne accounts are being originated on the Discover network instead of Mastercard as of early 2026.

services.name: Credit-building positioning
services.detail: Marketed toward fair credit (roughly FICO 580-669); Capital One offers automatic credit-line reviews after the account has been open a few months.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns the same cash-back rates (into your account)

additional_cards.note: No cost to add trusted users: an easy way to consolidate household dining/grocery spend onto the flat 3% categories.

timeline.text: Capital One revived the SavorOne name for a new $39-annual-fee, fair-credit product, a separate card from the no-fee Savor (the old SavorOne, renamed in Oct 2024).
The original no-fee SavorOne was renamed Savor, freeing up the SavorOne name for this newer, unrelated fair-credit product.
